headline: Trump agrees to a two-week ceasefire with Iran
description: Iran, the United States and Israel said they reached a deal for a two-week ceasefire, with Tehran saying it would negotiate with the US in Islamabad beginning Friday.

headline: Trump's escalatory rhetoric raises questions over his mental health
Iran's 'tactical gesture' in releasing French hostages Cécile Kohler and Jacques Paris
The race to succeed Christine Lagarde as president of the ECB begins
description: The president of the European Central Bank has indicated that she is considering leaving her post before her mandate ends in October 2027. Three candidates are preparing to step forward, while France may be eyeing the position of chief economist.

headline: French high-speed train and truck collide in another railway crossing tragedy
description: An accident at a railway crossing in the northern town of Bully-les-Mines on Tuesday resulted in the death of the train driver. The truck driver has been placed in police custody.

headline: At the Philharmonie de Paris, video game music takes center stage in a new exhibition
description: With the opening of 'Video Games & Music: la musique dont vous êtes le héros' ('The Music Where You Are the Hero') on April 2, the Parisian institution celebrates the rich connections between these two forms of expression.

headline: In catacombs, museums, and labs, the delicate heritage of human remains
description: The Paris Catacombs reopen to the public on Wednesday, April 8, after five months of renovations. The timing offers a chance to reflect on how human remains are handled in cultural and research institutions. These singular relics pose ethical, technical and legal questions.

Are Trump's ideologues intellectuals or imposters?
description: Curtis Yarvin has become a leading figure in the Neo-Reactionary movement (also known as the Dark Enlightenment), which has now made it to the White House. But can this blogger really be considered an intellectual? Political theorist Arnaud Miranda and sociologist Olivier Alexandre debate the origins and roles of one of the thinkers who has Trump's ear.

headline: Viktor Medvedchuk, the Ukrainian traitor Putin wanted to install in place of Zelensky
description: Captured in April 2022 as he tried to flee Ukraine, the former MP and oligarch now lives in exile in Russia. As a lawyer in Kyiv in 1980, he sent his client, the great Ukrainian poet Vasyl Stus, to his death.

headline: Pre-election Hungary's mood is one of twilight for Orban's rule
description: After 16 years of unchallenged rule, Hungary's ultra-conservative prime minister is, for the first time, seeing his supremacy challenged. With days until the April 12 legislative elections, pro-European challenger Peter Magyar is leading in the polls. In the ruling party's eastern strongholds, huge Chinese battery factories have sparked public backlash.
